answer:
The uncertainty principle states \(\Delta x \Delta p \ge \frac{h}{4\pi}\).  
Given \(\Delta x = 10 \times 10^{-12} \, \mathrm{m}\), the minimum uncertainty in momentum is \(\Delta p = \frac{h}{4\pi \Delta x} \approx 5.27 \times 10^{-24} \, \mathrm{kg \cdot m/s}\), but the options are an order of magnitude larger, suggesting they used \(\Delta p \approx \frac{h}{2\pi \Delta x}\) or a similar approximation, giving about \(1.05 \times 10^{-23} \, \mathrm{kg \cdot m/s}\).  
The closest match among the choices is \(5.0 \times 10^{-23} \, \mathrm{kg \cdot m/s}\), which corresponds to option C.

\boxed{C}
